title: Shared Observation Provenance Contract
description: Provenance, period and missing-value semantics shared by every fact_* observation family (indicator, trade, port/transport, cost). This file defines no standalone record; it is referenced by the four observation schemas so that one change to provenance rules applies to all of them. Mode-neutral by construction: nothing here assumes Ocean, so Air, Road, Rail and Border modules reuse it unchanged.

evidenceClass.description: Evidence classification per docs/thailand_multimodal_logistics_intelligence_scope.md Section 3.5, extended with the two fixture classes required by WO-010 Section 8 so that non-live data can never be mistaken for a live published observation.

valueStatus.description: Explicit missing-value status. Missing data must remain null or explicitly unavailable and must never be converted to zero: only 'available' permits a non-null value, and every other status forces value to be null.

transportMode.description: Mode reference. 'multimodal' and 'not_applicable' exist so shared entities never force an Ocean-only reading.

provenance.description: Required provenance block. Every published material fact must be traceable to a source, a data period, a publication time and a retrieval time.

record_id.description: Stable record ID. Deterministic for a given source, series and period so that re-collection updates rather than duplicates.

source_record_id.description: The source's own identifier for this record where one exists. Null when the source publishes no per-record identifier -- null is an honest absence, not an empty string.

published_at.description: When the source published this value. Null when the source states no publication time.

revised_at.description: When the source last revised this value, when the source exposes revision information.

revision_number.description: Monotonic counter incremented when a later collection changes the value for the same record_id. Revisions are preserved as separate history rows, never silently overwritten.

measurement.description: Value, unit and missing-value semantics. Enforced by scripts/validate.py: value is non-null if and only if value_status is 'available'.

unit.description: Unit of measure, e.g. 'THB_million', 'USD', 'teu', 'index_points', 'port_calls', 'THB_per_litre'. Null only when value_status is not 'available'.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency for monetary values; null for non-monetary series.

placement.description: Where the observation applies. Every field is nullable because a valid observation may be country-level with no node, or node-level with no lane.
